tldr: Adyen and Square are both PSPs but Square is SMB-focused with strong omnichannel POS, online checkout, and invoicing. The migration is a vault transfer plus a rewrite against Square's APIs, and is best suited to teams whose payment volume and feature needs fit Square's SMB profile.
why_switch: Strong in-store POS and reader hardware
Invoicing and online checkout in one platform
what_to_export: Customer payment methods and recurring agreements
Catalog or product references tied to payments
export_method: Coordinate a PCI-DSS-compliant vault transfer between Adyen and Square via the standard PSP-to-PSP / network-token process; this is contractual and not a client-side data export. In parallel, export shopper references, recurring agreements, refund/dispute history, and settlement reports from Adyen using its reporting tools. Save webhook configurations and signing keys. Capture the Adyen merchant account and store hierarchy because Square models multi-location setups differently (locations within a Square account). Keep finance reports as your audit trail.
import_method: Onboard a Square account, complete KYC, and set up locations matching your Adyen store structure. Initiate the card vault transfer with both PSPs. Re-integrate against Square's Payments API (Web Payments SDK on the client, server SDKs for charges and refunds), including 3DS where needed. Re-implement webhook handlers against Square's event format and signature verification. If you use Square for in-person payments, integrate readers and the Terminal API. Test in sandbox, run a low-traffic canary, then ramp. Keep Adyen for in-flight disputes and refunds against pre-migration charges.
effort: 2-4 months including KYC, vault transfer, and 3DS testing
gotchas: Square has narrower country coverage than Adyen; verify availability per market
Card vault transfer is contractual and PCI-controlled; cannot be client-driven
Per-country local payment methods may not match Adyen's coverage
3DS/SCA flows must be re-implemented and tested per region
Refunds and disputes on pre-migration charges still flow through Adyen
Square's pricing model and fee structure differ; rerun unit economics

email.subject: Migrating PSP from Adyen to Square — kickoff plan
email.body:
Hi team,

We are evaluating a move from Adyen to Square. The end-to-end project runs longer than two weeks because of KYC and the card-vault transfer, but here is the 14-day kickoff plan.

Days 1-3: confirm Square covers our markets and SMB feature needs, complete onboarding and KYC.
Days 4-7: kick off PCI-compliant vault transfer with both PSPs and our compliance team.
Days 8-11: rebuild integration on Web Payments SDK and server SDK in sandbox, including 3DS and webhooks.
Days 12-14: run sandbox QA across representative scenarios and finalize the canary plan.

Full production cutover will follow once vault transfer and 3DS testing are complete.

Thanks,
Your customer
